Part B.key_evidence: Faint positive g-band detection (magpsf~21.26) coincident (distpsnr1=0.19") with a PS1 source flagged as a galaxy (sgscore1=0.06) with PS1 mags ~21 in g/r and brighter in i/z (consistent with a galaxy). ndethist=9 over ncovhist=1164 visits indicates repeated detections at the same position. classtar=0.97 suggests point-like morphology of the current detection. Cutouts are noisy but show no obvious artifact pattern; the source sits on an extended/faint host.
Part B.leading_interpretation_and_support: Most consistent with AGN: a point-like nuclear variable (distpsnr1~0.2", centered on a likely galaxy per sgscore1=0.06) with multiple historical detections (ndethist=9) over a long monitoring baseline and low-amplitude variability typical of AGN nuclei.
Part B.alternative_analysis: A supernova is possible given a new positive residual on a galaxy, but the near-zero offset from the PS1 galaxy nucleus and the repeated prior detections over many epochs favor persistent nuclear variability (AGN) rather than a one-off transient.
